claims.claim: Pete posted SO #14456547 on 2013-01-22 asking how to unit test a Jersey MultiPart handler without Jersey-Test or Grizzly
Pete's test threw ClassCastException: [B cannot be cast to com.sun.jersey.multipart.BodyPartEntity
Pete's fallback using getEntityAs(BodyPartEntity.class) threw IllegalStateException: Entity instance does not contain the unconverted content
The reproduction uses Jersey 1.19.4 (jersey-multipart, jersey-core, jersey-server) and JUnit 4.13.2
The production cast line (BodyPartEntity) multiPart.getBodyParts().get(0).getEntity() is reproduced verbatim in MultiPartReader.java
Two tests reproduce both failure modes without expected= so exceptions surface as raw errors
mvn test produces Tests run: 2, Failures: 0, Errors: 2 with both ClassCastException and IllegalStateException
Root cause: in-process MultiPart construction stores byte[] as entity; Jersey's MessageBodyReader is never invoked so BodyPartEntity is never created
Lark forensic validation failed three times with Invocation limit reached — not a code defect
Lark workflow group ID is wfl_grp_MtuxpVJdco7hwfxMsv9Irrv8, final execution ID is wflw_exec_0DhelB7LxYizOvXsFmS1pNwZ
Case status is leads_exhausted / Partial; Lark verification never ran
jersey-server is on the classpath only to satisfy RuntimeDelegate SPI lookup; not exercised by test logic
